title: campanula rapunculus subvar. verruculosa
intro: Campanula rapunculus subvar. verruculosa, also known as the rough-leaved rampion, is a perennial plant species within the Campanula rapunculus genus. It has been used in traditional medicine for its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties, particularly in treating wounds and skin irritations. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, alleviating respiratory issues, and supporting the immune system, although more research is needed to confirm these claims. In herbal preparations, Campanula rapunculus subvar. verruculosa is often used as a tincture, infusion, or salve, although it's essential to consult with a healthcare professional before using it for medicinal purposes.
